heroHeadline: US-Iran Two-Week Ceasefire; Hormuz Reopens; Oil Crashes 16%; Vance Leads Islamabad Talks Apr 10; Trump Threatens 50% Tariffs on Iran Arms Suppliers; NATO Punishment Plan Leaked
heroSubtitle: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on April 8, brokered by Pakistan, with Iran opening the Strait of Hormuz under the deal — sending oil prices crashing 16% (WTI to $94/barrel). VP Vance will lead the US delegation to Islamabad peace talks on April 10, the first direct US-Iran engagement since Operation Epic Fury began February 28. However, the ceasefire is immediately disputed: Israel bombed Lebanon (254 killed), Iran accused the US of three ceasefire violations, and Hormuz status remains contested. Trump announced 50% tariffs on any country supplying Iran with military weapons (targeting China and Russia), with no legal authority cited post-SCOTUS IEEPA ruling. The WSJ reported Trump is weighing pulling US troops from 'unhelpful' NATO allies (Germany, Spain) while boosting presence in Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, SCOTUSblog, NPR, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, PBS NewsHour.
